what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that links the design-assets channel to an image asset identified by the URI image://design-assets/icons/VR_Gold.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content
It declares an attachment of type images
The target is an image:// URI rather than a filesystem path
It records an ISO 8601 added timestamp
The id is att-1df8f09e and the channel is design-assets
standouts: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address the attached asset
Extremely minimal metadata record with no embedded media data
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
concerns: No checksum, size, MIME type, or filename recorded for the image
The image:// URI cannot be resolved from this file alone
No recorded author or provenance for the attachment
notes: This is a bare attachment ledger entry pointing the design-assets channel to a VR_Gold icon image via a URI.
questions: What file format and dimensions does the VR_Gold icon have?
What system resolves the image://design-assets/icons/VR_Gold URI?
Who added this attachment and for what specific design purpose?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (record an image attachment for a channel) matches the content; however, the file is only a metadata stub and contains no actual image data.
intention_signals: Track which image assets are attached to the design-assets channel and when they were added, enabling discovery without embedding binary content.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to target the attached asset instead of a conventional file path or URL.
contribution: Provides a lightweight, timestamped index entry that maps a channel to an image asset via a URI, supporting attachment discovery and referencing without storing binary data.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata record in the design-assets channel pointing to the image asset image://design-assets/icons/VR_Gold, created on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image attachment for the design-assets channel so it can be referenced and retrieved by its URI.
